title: Helictotrichon parlatorei
taxonomy: Helictotrichon parlatorei is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It belongs to the order Poales, which includes various types of grasses and sedges. The genus Helictotrichon is part of the subfamily Pooideae, characterized by its grass-like appearance. Helictotrichon parlatorei is further classified as a member of the tribe Poeae, a group of grasses that includes many ornamental species. This classification provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.
uses: Helictotrichon parlatorei, also known as Parlatore's oat grass, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites in its leaves and stems.
